blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
If you are looking to play **Blind Man's Bluff** (also commonly known as **Indian Poker** or **Forehead Poker**) on your Android device, there are several real, dedicated apps available on the Google Play Store that capture this unique psychological card game:

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game**
* **Overview:** This is a highly polished, single-player adaptation of Blind Man's Bluff. 
* **How it works:** You play head-to-head against an AI opponent. You can see the AI's card, but your own card is hidden from you. You must read the AI's "expressions" and betting patterns to guess whether your card is higher or lower. 
* **Features:** 
  * Three AI difficulty levels (Easy, Normal, and Hard).
  * An interactive expression system where you can make your avatar smirk, sweat, or show anger to bluff the AI.
  * 100% offline play with no internet connection required.

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Overview:** A multiplayer-focused version of the game designed to be played with friends and family.
* **How it works:** True to the classic rules of Blind Man's Bluff, you have to predict your victory by looking only at your opponent's card. 
* **Features:** 
  * Local and online multiplayer options to play with friends.
  * Focuses heavily on psychological warfare, reading opponent expressions, and strategic betting.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Overview:** A lightweight, straightforward simulator of the classic card game.
* **How it works:** It acts as a digital deck and interface to facilitate playing the physical "card-on-the-forehead" game with a group of people in the same room.
* **Features:** 
  * Clean, no-nonsense interface.
  * Best used as a party tool when you want to play the game with a group of friends but don't have a physical deck of cards on hand.

forehead poker app Android.response:
If you are looking to play **Forehead Poker**—also widely known as **Indian Poker** or **Blind Man's Bluff** (where you see everyone else's cards but hold your own card to your forehead without looking at it)—there are several real, existing apps available on the Google Play Store for Android. 

The best Android apps for this style of gameplay, as well as general "forehead-style" party games, include:

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game**
* **Description:** This is a dedicated single-player psychological card game built entirely around the rules of Blind Man's Bluff (Indian Poker). Both you and an AI opponent receive a card; you can see the AI's card, but you cannot see your own. You have to read the AI's facial expressions and betting patterns across three difficulty levels (Easy, Normal, and Hard) to guess your card's value and decide whether to Call, Raise, Fold, or go All-In. 

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Description:** This app is designed for playing the classic Indian Poker game with friends and family. The gameplay focuses on psychological warfare—you must predict your victory by looking only at your opponent's card, reading their facial expressions, and managing your bets to bluff them out of the pot.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Description:** A lightweight, straightforward multiplayer simulation app designed to let a group of people play Indian Poker together using virtual playing cards. It acts as a digital deck of cards, making it easy to host a local game of "card-on-forehead" poker without needing a physical deck.

---

### Alternative: Forehead Guessing Games
If you meant "forehead poker" in the sense of the popular party game where you hold the phone to your forehead to guess words, you should look at:

* **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises): The definitive party game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. You place the phone on your forehead, and your friends must act out or give clues so you can guess the word on the screen before the timer runs out. 
* **Charades & Headbands Game** (by Juasapp): Another highly-rated party app where you place the device on your forehead and guess words, characters, or movies based on your friends' clues, dancing, or singing.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
If you are looking for an Android app featuring the unique mechanic where you cannot see your own cards (but can see everyone else's), you are likely looking for games based on **Hanabi** (a cooperative deduction game) or **Indian Poker / Blind Man's Bluff** (a competitive betting game). 

The following real, existing Android apps on the Google Play Store feature this exact gameplay mechanic:

### 1. Boardible: Games for Groups
* **Developer:** Boardible LTDA
* **Description:** This is a multiplayer board game platform that features a fully playable, officially licensed digital version of **Hanabi**. In Hanabi, you play as distracted pyrotechnicians trying to launch a spectacular fireworks show. Because you cannot see your own hand, you must rely entirely on the clues your teammates give you about the colors and numbers of your cards to figure out what to play. It supports both local and online matchmaking.

### 2. Indian Poker - Card Game
* **Developer:** Wooah Tech Inc
* **Description:** This app is a dedicated mobile version of **Indian Poker** (also known as Blind Man's Bluff). In this game, your card is hidden from you but visible to your opponents (simulating holding the card to your forehead). You must look at your opponents' cards, read their facial expressions, and use psychological bluffing to predict whether your hidden card is higher or lower than theirs before placing a bet.

### 3. Indian poker
* **Developer:** Kanbe
* **Description:** A lightweight, straightforward simulation app designed for playing the classic "card-on-forehead" Indian Poker game with multiple people. It is a great, ad-supported option if you want a simple interface to play the game with friends using your mobile devices.

### 4. Hanabi Tracker
* **Developer:** Robert Palazzo
* **Description:** If you already play the physical tabletop version of Hanabi with friends, this is a highly-rated companion app. Because the hardest part of the game is remembering the clues your friends have given you about your hidden hand, this utility app acts as a digital tracker. It lets you log the colors and numbers of your cards as you receive hints, freeing up your memory to focus on strategy.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
Depending on whether you are looking for a **party guessing game** (where your phone acts as the "card" on your forehead) or a **traditional playing card game** (like Indian Poker, where you hold a physical card to your head), there are several great options available on the Google Play Store:

### 1. Party & Charades Games (Phone on Forehead)
In these games, you select a category, hold your Android device to your forehead facing your friends, and try to guess the words on the screen using their clues.

*   **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises): The most famous and popular app of this genre, created by Ellen DeGeneres. It features dozens of themed decks (like Blockbuster Movies, Animals, and Accents). You tilt the phone down when you guess correctly and tilt it up to pass. 
    *   *Note: There is also a **Heads Up! Netflix Edition** available for active Netflix subscribers.*
*   **Charades!** (by FatChicken Studios): A highly rated, free alternative that offers over 45 themed decks. It includes categories ranging from TV shows and science to active challenges like "Dance Moves" and "Act It Out".
*   **Charades & Headbands: Guess Up** (by Cosmicode): Another excellent party game designed for group gatherings. It features a "Team Mode" for competitive play, supports multiple languages, and even lets you record videos of your friends acting out the clues.

---

### 2. Traditional Card Games (Indian Poker / Blind Man's Bluff)
If you are looking for the classic card game where players are dealt a standard playing card, hold it to their forehead without looking at it, and bet based on the cards they see on everyone else's foreheads:

*   **Blindex**: A dedicated, offline single-player "Blind Man's Bluff" (Indian Poker) game. You hold your virtual card to your head, try to read the AI opponents' tells, and bet across three different difficulty levels.
*   **Indian poker** (by Kanbe): A simple, straightforward multiplayer simulation of the classic forehead card game using standard playing cards.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
Here are some of the best real, existing Android apps available on the Google Play Store that let you play bluffing card games single-player and completely offline against AI:

### 1. **Cheat! – Bluffing Card Game**
This is a dedicated adaptation of the classic card game "Cheat" (also known as Bluff, BS, or I Doubt It). It is specifically designed for offline play, putting you in intense 4-player battles against 3 CPU opponents. 
* **Key Features:** Features a title system (Liar, Honest, or Hunter) that adapts to your playstyle, strategic power-up items (like Lie Counters and Bluff Shields), and daily missions.

### 2. **Bluff Card Game Offline** (by Vertex)
A highly-rated offline card game designed specifically to be played anytime, anywhere without an internet connection. It perfectly replicates the classic rules of "I Doubt It" and "Cheat".
* **Key Features:** Offers a 2-player mode (1v1 against a smart AI) and a 4-player mode for full-table action. It features dynamic AI difficulty, smooth animations, and detailed profile statistics to track your win/loss record.

### 3. **Bluff Master 420 - I Doubt It** (by Gametion)
Developed by the creators of Ludo King, this app brings the popular Indian variation of the bluffing card game (often called Bluffmaster) to your phone. 
* **Key Features:** Includes a dedicated offline Single Player mode where you can brush up on your deception skills against AI. It also features multiple visual themes and an easy step-by-step tutorial if you need a rules refresher.

### 4. **Liar's Dice Online Multiplayer** (by YOAMB B.V.)
Though traditionally played with dice rather than cards, *Liar's Dice* (also known as Perudo or Pirate Dice) is one of the most famous bluffing games in the world. 
* **Key Features:** While it has online multiplayer, the app features a robust **Offline Practice** mode where you can play against highly intelligent AI opponents offline. It is a fantastic option if you want to master the math and psychology of bluffing.

### 5. **Underhand** (by Spoopy Squad)
If you want a unique, narrative-driven twist on the genre, *Underhand* is a brilliant, atmospheric single-player offline card game. You play as a cult leader managing resources and responding to event cards.
* **Key Features:** The gameplay revolves around making "underhanded" choices, managing suspicion, and bluffing your way through encounters with the police and rival factions to summon ancient deities. It is entirely offline and built from the ground up for solo play.

* **Genre:** Poker Roguelike / Deckbuilder
* **Description:** *Balatro* is a highly addictive, poker-themed roguelike where you play illegal poker hands to score points and beat "Boss Blinds." The magic of the game lies in collecting game-changing Joker cards, tarot cards, and planet cards that break the rules of traditional poker and trigger massive point multipliers. It is a premium game that runs entirely offline and is perfect for quick or long sessions.

### 2. **Slay the Spire**
* **Genre:** Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Description:** Widely considered the gold standard of the deckbuilding genre, *Slay the Spire* tasks you with climbing a procedurally generated tower. You choose from four distinct characters, each with their own unique card pool and playstyle, to draft a deck, fight monsters, and collect powerful relics. It is a premium, ad-free, and fully offline experience.

### 3. **Dawncaster: Deckbuilding RPG**
* **Genre:** Dark Fantasy RPG Deckbuilder
* **Description:** Designed specifically for mobile, *Dawncaster* is played in portrait mode and features a dark fantasy aesthetic. It offers over 900 hand-illustrated cards and multiple classes (like Rogue, Warrior, and Seeker). It is a premium game with no microtransactions, no ads, and no internet connection required. 

### 4. **Night of the Full Moon**
* **Genre:** Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Description:** Based on the story of Little Red Riding Hood, this game features a beautiful, dark-fairytale art style. You choose a class, venture into the Black Forest, and build your deck as you encounter various monsters and story events. It is played vertically, making it great for one-handed play, and is fully playable offline.

### 5. **Pirates Outlaws**
* **Genre:** Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Description:** If you enjoy *Slay the Spire* but want a different theme, *Pirates Outlaws* is an excellent choice. You navigate various seas, manage your "ammo" resource to play cards, and battle outlaw pirates. It features multiple game modes, over 15 characters to unlock, and hundreds of cards to collect—all playable offline.

### 6. **Card Thief**
* **Genre:** Stealth Solitaire / Puzzle
* **Description:** *Card Thief* reimagines classic solitaire as a tactical stealth game. You play as a thief moving through a 3x3 grid of cards representing guards, torches, and treasures. Your goal is to manage your stealth points, extinguish lights, pickpocket guards, and escape with the loot. It is a highly strategic, free-to-play game with a one-time unlock for the full version.

best offline single player card game And.response:
If you want the **best offline single-player card games on Android**, the strongest picks are **Card Crawl** and **Solitaire**—both work well without an internet connection and are easy to play in short sessions.

- **Card Crawl** — A clever **single-player dungeon card game** with quick runs, strategy, and replayability.
- **Solitaire** — The classic **Klondike-style** offline card game; simple, familiar, and dependable.
- **Pirate Outlaws** — A **deck-building roguelike** with offline play and more depth than standard solitaire.
- **Solitaire Grand Harvest** — A polished **card-puzzle** style game that can be played offline in many modes.
- **Ascension** — A well-known **deck-building card game** with strong solo play and tactical decisions.
- **Shattered Pixel Dungeon** — Not a traditional card game, but if you meant a **single-player offline strategy game with cards/deck management**, it is a strong Android option.

If you want the **best overall choice**, I’d start with **Card Crawl** for strategy, or **Solitaire** if you want the most familiar offline experience.
